propositions: Retired but still-set env vars and TOML keys are silently ignored or defaulted, redirecting the store or weakening security/lifecycle policy without any operator signal.

A RemovedInput tombstone registry maps retired env/cfg/file inputs to a targeted migration error naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version, with nonzero exit for behavior-affecting inputs and warn-only for inert ones.
RemovedInputError must subclass BaseException so it is not swallowed by tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError or the MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.
Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks fire at the top of load_config() before the cache lookup so they trigger on a warm MCP cache hit, with tombstoned names added to _env_signature().
A non-raising scan_tombstones function backs rebar config validate / doctor to report all tombstone/alias matches, exiting non-zero when any error-class input is present.
A CI golden-file guard fails if a recognised config key or env var is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry, keeping the registry complete by construction.
